prompt.role: Senior Python AI Engineer (LLM Systems & Context Management)
prompt.task:
Implement a context limitation mechanism for chat history (pseudo-memory) to prevent excessive growth and ensure efficient interaction with the LLM.
Requirements 1. Add Context Limitation
Implement ONE (or both) of the following strategies:
Option A — Limit by message count Keep only the last N messages (e.g., 10–20) Configurable limit (e.g., MAX_HISTORY_MESSAGES) Option B — Limit by total text length Limit total characters or tokens in history Configurable limit (e.g., MAX_HISTORY_CHARS) 2. Overflow Handling
When the limit is exceeded:
Remove oldest messages first (FIFO approach) Ensure the newest messages are always preserved 3. Integration
Update history handling logic:
Apply trimming: after adding a new message or before sending history to LLM Must work per user (independent histories) 4. Data Format (unchanged)
History format must remain:
[ { "role": "user", "content": "..." }, { "role": "assistant", "content": "..." } ]
✅ Definition of Done Context size is limited according to selected strategy Old messages are correctly removed New messages are always preserved History works independently per user No errors when sending history to LLM Code is extensible for future strategies 🧪 Validation Steps Send more than 20 messages → verify only last N are kept Ensure oldest messages are removed first Test with multiple users (independent histories) Verify LLM responses remain correct 💡 Optional Improvements Add strategy switch (count / length) Add logging for trimming operations Add future support for history summarization 📁 Suggested File
src/history.py
Functions to implement/update:
append_message() get_history() trim_history() clear_history()
